nodes.label: ADR-0002: Per-Conference D1 Tenancy
nodes.rationale: A Conference is the natural administrative boundary â€” Conference officers see all their churches' data; members and churches in different Conferences never interact. Per-Conference isolation simplifies access control (row-level security per church_id within the database rather than cross-tenant filters), limits blast radius, and maps to D1's horizontal scaling model (thousands of small databases are free; one massive database hits the 10GB ceiling). A typical 500-church Conference with 10 years of transactions fits in under 1GB. Rejected per-church D1 (NÃ— complexity for cross-church operations) and single global D1 (D1 single-threaded throughput limits and 10GB ceiling).
